page: Amazon Web Services m6i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting hyper-threading with two threads per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with the instance scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is also a notable strength, achieving top 25% results in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 10% due to hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and VAES. Single-core performance, however, remains in the middle 50% across stress-ng and Geekbench single-core evaluations.

The instance demonstrates distinct performance profiles depending on the workload. It excels in database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, all of which register in the top 25% of benchmark results. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware supports prompt processing for small and medium models efficiently, but text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom 25% due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This makes the instance suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded web servers, and database hosting, while being less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for multi-core CPU tests, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside a top 10% rating in CPU encryption. Conversely, single-core metrics and large-scale LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, with the latter falling into the bottom 25%. This profile makes the m6i.24xlarge highly suitable for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database hosting, and static web serving, while being less efficient for large-scale AI inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute platform featuring 96 virtual cores and 384 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors.
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 25%.
Top-tier cryptographic execution utilizing hardware-accelerated AES and VAES.
Baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps without local storage.
Well-suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving.
Performance tradeoff in large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU.
